  "weights": {

  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which primary focus should I take for this section: (A) sentence- and paragraph-level editing for clarity/conciseness (R1.6), (B) reframing and tightening the core contribution and argument logic (R1.1/R1.2), or (C) checking that claims are properly supported and cited (evidence-claim alignment and citation accuracy; R1.3/R1.7)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Conceptual reframing may be needed to crisply state the core contribution and how the grounding supports it.",
    "R1.2": "The argument from benchmark results to default method choice to system contribution should be checked for internal coherence.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about method performance and evaluation outcomes should align with what is reported in the section.",
    "R1.6": "Line-level polishing can improve clarity, concision, and flow without changing the section’s structure.",
    "R1.7": "Accurate and sufficient citation of the benchmark and related attributions is important for credibility.",
    "RX.1": "Explicitly conveying limits and uncertainties helps appropriately qualify the presented claims.",
    "RX.2": "Avoiding overstatement ensures the section does not generalize beyond what the evidence and citations support.",
    "RX.3": "Staying within the scope of assisting this specific section prevents unintended changes elsewhere.",
    "RX.4": "Careful wording reduces the risk of readers adopting misinformed practices based on overstated claims.",
    "RX.5": "Clear linkage between each claim and its source supports traceability and auditability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which primary focus should I take for this section: (A) sentence- and paragraph-level editing for clarity/conciseness (R1.6), (B) reframing and tightening the core contribution and argument logic (R1.1/R1.2), or (C) checking that claims are properly supported and cited (evidence-claim alignment and citation accuracy; R1.3/R1.7)?

rationale.R1.1: Conceptual reframing may be needed to crisply state the core contribution and how the grounding supports it.
rationale.R1.2: The argument from benchmark results to default method choice to system contribution should be checked for internal coherence.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about method performance and evaluation outcomes should align with what is reported in the section.
rationale.R1.6: Line-level polishing can improve clarity, concision, and flow without changing the section’s structure.
rationale.R1.7: Accurate and sufficient citation of the benchmark and related attributions is important for credibility.
rationale.RX.1: Explicitly conveying limits and uncertainties helps appropriately qualify the presented claims.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overstatement ensures the section does not generalize beyond what the evidence and citations support.
rationale.RX.3: Staying within the scope of assisting this specific section prevents unintended changes elsewhere.
rationale.RX.4: Careful wording reduces the risk of readers adopting misinformed practices based on overstated claims.
rationale.RX.5: Clear linkage between each claim and its source supports traceability and auditability.
